drop_reason: Confirmed-fatal scoring trap: all 8 FAIL_TO_PASS tests already PASS on the base commit cc8c7f5 with zero agent work (verified by running `bun test src/scheduler/scheduler.test.ts src/tests/scheduled-tasks.test.ts` on a fresh clone — 31/31 pass, 8/8 F2P PASSED, simulated test.sh reward = 1.0000). The F2P names are pre-existing recurring-schedule tests (`calculateNextRun::calculates next run with cron expression`, `should create a scheduled task with cron expression`, etc.) that have nothing to do with one-time-schedule behavior. The canonical patch is genuinely markdown-only: all 10 mutating ops in original_session.json (msgs 60, 62, 79, 81, 83, 85, 87, 90, 92, 94) write/edit only `thoughts/taras/plans/2026-03-06-one-time-scheduled-tasks.md`. The original session was a `/desplega:create-plan` invocation in Autopilot mode that produced a 353-line design doc, not an implementation. No code-mutating ops were missed by extraction.
notes:
Triple-check rescue paths considered and rejected:

1. REPAIR CANONICAL PATCH — rejected. Audited all 97 messages in original_session.json. Every mutating op (10 total: 2 Write, 8 Edit) targets the same plan markdown file. No Bash heredocs writing code, no ts/sql files touched. The session genuinely was planning-only. The 5 reconstruction warnings are harmless (`old text not found` on later Edit ops to the markdown file).

2. PLANNING-ONLY BENCHMARK (grade markdown) — rejected. Would require (a) discarding the SWE-rebench JUnit test.sh template all sibling tasks use, (b) writing a structural rubric (sections, key terms like `scheduleType`/`delayMs`/`runAt`) easily gamed by any agent that read the user's 'maybe adding a new column' hint, (c) measuring design-doc quality which is orthogonal to this benchmark's stated purpose (multi-turn coding correction). Worse, the user_simulation_prompt.md target is exactly 2 messages and then silence — there's no multi-turn correction signal to measure even if the rubric existed.

3. KEEP PAIRED WITH 4a881b AS STEP 1 — rejected. They're separate sessions from different agents starting from the same base commit. Welding c020d6's plan-writing into 4a881b's implementation would be synthetic, not derived from a real multi-turn session. 4a881b already stands alone with proper F2P tests on the actual feature contract.

4. BORROW 4a881b's TESTS — rejected. The c020d6 canonical patch (markdown-only) cannot satisfy 4a881b's F2P (which require `scheduleType` column, `delayMs`/`runAt` params, auto-disable logic, etc.). There's no gold standard for c020d6 under that test set, so no fidelity check is possible.

Verification commands run (all reproducible):
  - cloned desplega-ai/agent-swarm @ cc8c7f59bb517e0e88262a059d9023f3913f37a3
  - `bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun test --reporter=junit src/scheduler/scheduler.test.ts src/tests/scheduled-tasks.test.ts` -> 31 pass / 0 fail
  - simulated test.sh parse: parse_combined_test_reports yielded all 8 F2P as PASSED -> reward 1.0000 on the buggy base
  - confirmed thoughts/taras/plans/2026-03-06-one-time-scheduled-tasks.md does land in merged commit 58f2aa27 (373 lines, near-identical to canonical patch's 353)

Conclusion: c020d6 cannot be rescued. The verifier scores 1.0 for doing nothing, the canonical patch contains no code, and 4a881b already covers the implementation slice of this same upstream commit. Prior DROP verdict is correct.
